Bankroll: The specific amount of money you set aside strictly for playing poker.
Bet: Putting chips into the pot when you're the first to commit on a betting round.
Big Blind: The larger forced bet posted by the player two seats left of the dealer.
Big Slick: A nickname for Ace-King (AK). One of the most famous hands.
Blinds: Forced bets by the small and big blinds that seed the pot before cards go out.
Buy-in: The amount of money you pay to receive chips at a table.
Call: Matching the previous player's bet to stay in the hand.
Cash Games: A format where chips have real money value and you can join or leave at will.
Full Ring: A poker table with 9 or 10 players. Requires tighter play from early positions.
Dealer Button: The position that acts last post-flop. The most profitable seat.
Loose: A style of play where you play many different hands.
Check: Passing the action without betting when no one has bet yet.
Chip: A token representing money on the poker table.
Community Cards: Cards dealt face up on the table that everyone shares.
GTO: Game Theory Optimal. Playing in a way that cannot be exploited over the long run.
Preflop Charts: Cheat sheets that map hands to actions for every position.
Range: The specific set of hands a player acts with in a given situation.
Suited Connectors: Cards in sequence of the same suit (e.g., 7♠ 8♠). Great for making Straights and Flushes.
Suited: Two cards of the same suit. They have a higher chance of making a Flush.
Fold: Discarding your hand and surrendering the pot.
Flop: The first three community cards revealed after preflop betting.
Flush: Five cards of the exact same suit. Beats a Straight, but loses to a Full House.
Full House: Three cards of one rank plus a pair of another rank.
High Card: The highest card you hold when no other hand beats it; used to break ties.
Luck: The random factor that can help or hurt you from one hand to the next.
Pot: The pile of chips that everyone is fighting to win in a hand.
Offsuit: Two cards of different suits (e.g., a Heart and a Spade). They cannot make a Flush together.
Tight: A style of play where you fold most hands and only play strong ones.
Preflop: The betting round that happens before any community cards are dealt.
Quads: Four cards of the same rank.
Raise: Increasing the current bet and forcing opponents to invest more.
Rake: The small fee the casino takes from each pot to host the game.
River: The fifth and final community card dealt.
Pair: Two cards of the same rank.
Pocket Pair: When your two hole cards have the same rank (e.g., 7-7 or A-A).
Royal Flush: A-K-Q-J-10 of the same suit; the best possible hand.
Set: Three of a kind made when you hold a pocket pair.
Showdown: The final phase where remaining players reveal cards to determine the winner.
Hand Ranking: The hierarchy of poker hands (Flush beats Straight, etc.).
Kicker: The unmatched card in your hand. If two players have the same Pair, the one with the higher Kicker wins.
Skill: The ability to make mathematically sound decisions consistently.
Small Blind: The smaller forced bet posted immediately to the left of the dealer.
Stack: All the chips you have at the table.
Straight: Five cards in numerical order (e.g., 5-6-7-8-9). Beats Three of a Kind.
Straight Flush: Five consecutive cards of the same suit.
Tournaments: A format where players pay a buy-in and play until they win or bust.
Trips: Three of a kind made using one pocket card and two community cards.
Two Pair: A hand containing two different pairs.
Value Bet: A bet made with a strong hand, hoping the opponent calls with a worse hand.
6-Max: A poker table with a maximum of 6 players. Faster and more aggressive.
UTG: Under the Gun. The player who acts first pre-flop. A difficult position.
Position: Your seat relative to the dealer. Acting last is 'In Position' (Good). Acting first is 'Out of Position' (Bad).
Bluff: A bet made with a weak hand, hoping the opponent folds a better hand.
Variance: The natural swings in poker; even good players lose sometimes.
